Comment: Amazon Ads ingestion: expand 26 profiles x ad_products, fan out with a per-branch request/poll/download loop (Amazon Ads has no AWS SDK integration -- every ingestion step is a Lambda Task), then validate and load. ${...} tokens are deploy-time placeholders (Lambda ARNs, bucket/job names) -- substitute via whatever deploy tooling is chosen; not committed to a specific IaC framework in this repo, mirroring the reference POS Pipeline which also ships no Terraform/CDK.

PrepareMapInput.Comment: Expands config/profiles.yaml into Map items and computes the shared rolling report-date window, anchored on this execution's EventBridge event time.

IngestProfiles.Comment: Up to 26 profiles x 3 ad products (not all profiles run all ad products -- see README Open Items). MaxConcurrency capped well below the item count to respect Amazon's per-account report-request throttling. ToleratedFailurePercentage lets a handful of bad profile/ad_product combos fail without aborting the whole run -- the shared SNS alert still fires per branch failure via the rejected-object / CloudWatch-metric path, not by aborting this Map.

InitPollCount.Comment: Seed poll_count=0, explicitly carrying forward every field -- ASL Parameters replaces the whole state, it doesn't merge.

InitialWait.Comment: Reports rarely finish instantly; avoid a wasted poll immediately after request.

IncrementPollCount.Comment: ~30 iterations x 30-60s wait covers Amazon's ~15 min worst-case report generation time with margin.

ReportTimedOut.Comment: Amazon's own GitHub tracker documents reports stuck in PENDING indefinitely with no confirmed root cause -- this branch surfaces that as a distinct, alertable outcome rather than hanging forever. Emits a custom metric because ToleratedFailurePercentage on the enclosing Map means this branch failing does NOT turn the overall execution FAILED, so the EventBridge Execution-Status-Change rule alone would never see it -- see infra/configure_alerting.py's alarm on AdsPipeline/Ingestion BranchFailureCount.

IngestionBranchFailed.Comment: Same rationale as ReportTimedOut -- a tolerated per-branch failure needs its own alertable signal since it won't fail the overall execution.

ValidateBronze.Comment: AWS Glue Python Shell job, bronze -> silver/rejected. .sync suffix waits for job completion natively -- no custom poll loop needed here, unlike the Redshift load step.

LoadRedshift.Comment: Nested state machine (statemachine/redshift_load.asl.json) -- kept separate since its own poll loop (Redshift Data API has no .sync integration) is a distinct concern from ingestion's poll loop.

PipelineFailed.Comment: Terminal failure. An EventBridge rule on this state machine's Execution Status Change = FAILED/TIMED_OUT feeds the shared SNS topic -> AWS Chatbot -> Teams, same as the reference pipeline's alerting pattern -- see infra/configure_alerting.py.
